* A strategic thinker with leadership and business experience
* An entrepreneurial mind ready to operate independently with strong central support
* Someone who wants more than a job—they want ownership and impact


 You'll manage everything from operations to marketing to team hiring. But you won't do it alone—our HQ team provides the tools, playbook, and platform to help you succeed.
   

  

**This isn't a typical job - it's an opportunity to own and operate a business** 
 - for someone with an entrepreneurial mindset to take the reins, lead locally, and build a business that changes how people experience the beautiful game.
   

  

**Curious?**
 Visit www.strangersoccer.com → Click "Bring Stranger Soccer to Your City" to explore the opportunity and express your interest.
   

  

 Let's change how the world plays football—one city at a time.

title: Bagagetekniker till Swedavia, Stockholm Arlanda Airport
description:
Gillar du teknik, problemlösning och en arbetsplats där det alltid händer något? Då kan det här vara jobbet för dig! Nu söker Swedavia bagagetekniker till Stockholm Arlanda Airport, en av Sveriges mest samhällskritiska och spännande arbetsplatser.
   

  

 Swedavia har uppdraget att äga, underhålla och utveckla all infrastruktur på Arlanda, en samhällsviktig verksamhet som är öppen och i drift dygnet runt, året om vilket innebär att vi arbetar 3-skift. Vårt uppdrag är att leverera funktion, kapacitet och hög tillgänglighet till våra resenärer. Nu söker vi dig som har erfarenhet av mekanik, svets, el eller styr och reglerteknik.
   

  

**Vad innebär rollen?**
 Som Bagagetekniker är ditt huvudsakliga uppdrag att arbeta med tekniskt underhåll i våra bagageanläggningar. Du är en nyckelperson för att säkerställa att bagageanläggningen fungerar felfritt och i nära samarbete med andra aktörer samt olika delar av organisationen bidrar du till en säker och effektiv drift. I rollen ingår tunga lyft och arbete på hög höjd.
   

  

**Bland arbetsuppgifterna ingår**
* Förebyggande- och periodiskt underhåll.
* Hantera akuta felavhjälpning/felsökningar, återställning utav olika larm i bagageanläggningen.
* Förbättringsarbete


 I arbetet med våra bagageanläggningar kommer du i kontakt med elinstallationer, IT, styrsystem, mekanik och pneumatik. Även svetsning och byggarbete förekommer. Således kommer vi att använda oss av teoretiska och fysiska arbetstester under denna rekryteringsprocess.
   

  

**Vem är du?**
**Vi söker dig som har**
* Gymnasiekompetens inom relevant område eller annan motsvarande utbildning/erfarenhet.
* Teknisk erfarenhet inom exempelvis mekanik, el, PLC, svets, styr-och reglerteknik eller byggarbete.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska i tal och skrift.
* God datorvana.
* God fysik.
* Du är lyhörd och har lätt för att samarbeta, samt att du kan arbeta självständigt.
* Du är flexibel och stresstålig.
* Du kan prioritera viktiga och akuta arbetsuppgifter.
* Du har förmåga att strukturera upp ditt arbete.
* Kunna fatta egna beslut.
* B-körkort.


**Meriterande**
 Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av AI, tekniskt underhåll, felsökning och drift av större system och/eller tekniska anläggningar. Erfarenhet av arbete i komplexa miljöer med höga krav på tillgänglighet och säkerhet ses som en fördel.
   

  

**Vad erbjuder vi dig?**
 Vi erbjuder dig en rolig tjänst där det finns stora möjligheter att utveckla sina tekniska kunskaper inom flera olika områden.
   

  

 Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Jukka Björk, Jukka.bjork@swedavia.se.
   

  

**Sista ansökningsdag är den 4 maj, urval sker löpande och annonsen kan stängas tidigare så tveka inte att söka redan idag!**
**Hur är det att arbeta på Swedavia?**
 Vårt fokus är att möjliggöra hållbart resande och uppmuntra nytänkande för att skapa en välkomnande, trygg och inkluderande arbetsgemenskap där du kan växa och göra verklig skillnad – läs mer här! Jobba hos oss | Om Swedavia
   

  

 Då flygplatser är en säkerhetsklassad verksamhet genomgår samtliga kandidater som går vidare i rekryteringsprocessen en bakgrundskontroll. För vissa tjänster även en säkerhetsprövning.

title: Senior Credit Risk Analyst
description:
At Bank Norwegian and Nordax Bank, we believe in simplicity, transparency, and technology-driven innovation. As part of NOBA Bank Group, we offer consumer loans, credit cards, savings accounts and insurance – all made available through cutting-edge technology, automation, and seamless self-service platforms.
   

  

 With over 2 million customers across Norway, Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Germany, and Spain, we’re proud to be part of the largest independent specialist consumer finance lender in the Nordics – and we’re just getting started.
   

  

 Whether you're located in Oslo (Fornebu) or Stockholm (Gävlegatan 22), this is an opportunity to join a bank that is growing fast and shaping the future of consumer finance in Europe.
   

  

**About The Role**
 Our Credit & Analytics department is known for being one of the most forward-thinking in the Nordics, made up of talents from across the globe. Within this department, the Credit Underwriting & Decision Analytics unit focuses on continuously improving our automated underwriting processes and monitoring risk performance.
   

  

 As our unsecured portfolios across the Nordics grow in both size and complexity, we’re now looking for a Senior Credit Risk Analyst to join us on this exciting journey and help influence our future solutions. Credit underwriting is within the absolute core for a bank operating within unsecured credits. Working and coordinating with stakeholders from multiple departments is essential, and internally we simply refer to this role as having the day-to-day responsibility for “running the bank”
   

  

**Main Tasks**
* Having complete responsibility for a portfolio from a credit risk perspective
* Consistently analyze assigned portfolios to identify underwriting (UW) opportunities and risks, delivering actionable recommendations based on analysis and investigation
* Maintain a strong overview of the country portfolio, understanding key figures, trends and effects of UW changes
* Lead and coordinate cross-functional initiatives for underwriting changes, improvements and effectively managing input from key stakeholders
* Develop and enhance risk reporting using advanced analytical tools
* Engage in the bank’s model validation activities and ensure compliance with regulatory standards and internal guidelines
* Support strategic growth initiatives by conducting deep-dive analyses across products and markets
* Identify untapped data opportunities and turn them into actionable insights
* Collaborate on other tasks within Credit Underwriting & Decision Analytics to support our continuous improvement journey


**We Are Looking For You Who**
* Fluency in English (Scandinavian language skills is a plus)
* Minimum 5 years’ experience working within credit risk in the Nordic countries
* Strong communication skills – ability to explain complex findings in an understandable way
* Working experience from using analytical tools such R, Python, SAS or SQL/Databricks
* A degree in a quantitative field (Economics, Statistics, Data Science or similar)


**Nice To Have**
* Familiarity with reporting tools such as Tableau or Power BI
* Exposure to cloud platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure, GCP)
* Experience of working with B2B.
* Experience of working within the Norwegian or the Finnish market


**Who You Are**
 Knowing it’s a lot to ask for, but if you are creative, curious and have strong analytical skills at the same time, we think you could make a huge impact on our business. You are eager to use data and willing to challenge and improve existing methods. You enjoy both working independently and collaborating closely with others.
   

  

 In addition to this, it is important to us that you recognize yourself in our values: we are wholehearted, we collaborate and we take the lead.
   

  

 Have you heard of NOBAVERSE?
   

  

 In our NOBAVERSE, we value openness, trust, and diversity. Here, you can influence your own development and have fun along the way to achieving our goals – all in a workplace that’s a little extra
 *bubbly* 
 ! ✨
   

  

 Read more about our employee value proposition on our careers page.
   

  

**Read more about how our recruitment process works in the** 
**FAQ** 
**.**
 We care for your privacy and, with reference to the GDPR, would like to ask you not to include any sensitive personal data in your application, for examples information about ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs or information about your health.
   

  

**Read more about how we handle your personal data** 
**here** 
**.**
 If you have any questions or concerns about the position, please contact the responsible recruiter.
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application! 🙏✨

title: Product Manager - Analytics
description:
You know that moment when a simple staffing question suddenly turns into three tools, five messages, and a lot of guesswork?
   

  

 At Quinyx, we’re building a product that removes that friction - for managers, employees, and entire organisations. And we’re looking for a Product Manager who understands how valuable it is when software simply works and makes someone’s day easier.
   

  

 If you enjoy solving real operational challenges, shaping clear product direction, and working with a team that cares about thoughtful, scalable solutions, you’ll fit right in with us.
   

  

****🎯 About the role****
 Product Managers at Quinyx contribute to creating an innovative and intelligent WFM solution that we are proud of and have fun building. We help our customers become more productive, stay compliant with regulation and reach their business goals. As a Product Manager, you are fundamental in this goal, as it is your responsibility to ensure that your team is delivering the right product, with the right set of priorities, consistent with product vision and business goals.
   

  

****📝 What you’ll do****
* Own the evolution of the Quinyx Analytics capabilities, supporting the entire Quinyx product range with high-quality analytics through embedded reports, self-serve analytics & data-as-a-service
* Closely collaborate with your peers, cross-functional team and stakeholders to set the roadmap, product strategy, synthesize user and customer needs as well as drive the go-to-market to create commercial success.
* Verify market requirements that will add value for a significant part of a given vertical and market.
* Understanding and solving customer needs through interviewing users, analyzing data, forming hypotheses and trying them out in iterations.
* Work to define and analyze metrics, KPIs and ensure that the team is constantly doing the right things consistent with product vision and business goals.
* Harness the latest AI technologies to accelerate how you go about your work.


****✅ What we’re looking for****
* Experience working as a Product Manager with analytical products
* Experience working with cross-functional teams and collaborating with multiple stakeholders throughout the organisation.
* High capability of formulating and communicating product vision and strategy.
* Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills as well as an analytical and creative mindset.


**If you also possess the following, we consider it a plus:**
* Experience of WFM space, absence management or HR systems
* Entrepreneurial mindset
* Self-starter, willing to learn and improve


 💙 Why Quinyx?Quinyx Is a Leading Workforce Management Platform, Helping Businesses Simplify Scheduling, Optimize Staffing, And Engage Their Frontline Teams. We Empower Organizations Across The Globe—ranging From Retail And Hospitality To Logistics And Healthcare—with Smart, Mobile-first Solutions. At Quinyx, You’ll Find
   

  

* A truly international team with colleagues from all over the world 🌍
* A flexible and hybrid-friendly work culture 🧘
* Opportunities to grow, take ownership, and make real impact 📈
* An open, inclusive, and fun environment where your ideas matter 💡


****🎁 What we offer****
* Flexible work hours and a hybrid setup with at least 2 days from the office.
* Enhanced vacation allowance, gym membership contribution, health insurance and a pension plan.
* On-market pay.


 📬 How To Apply & Our ProcessReady To Join The Team? Submit Your Application Below, And We’ll Be In Touch As Soon As Possible. We’re Excited To Hear From You! What Our Hiring Process Looks Like
   

  

* Fill out a short application, no cover letter needed.
* Get invited to complete our psychometric and objective assessments through Alva Labs.
* Quick screening call with our Talent team.
* Meet the hiring manager.
* Case presentation (in-office).
* References & background checks.


****🙌 Quinyx is an equal opportunity employer****
 We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees—no matter your background, identity, or experience.
   

  

*****When work just works!*****

title: Senior Automation Manager
description:
We're looking for a Senior Automation Manager where you'll lead a team of Product Owners to drive the development and implementation of automation initiatives that enhance operational efficiency and service quality in our ticketing and fulfillment processes.
   

  

 This vacancy can be located at any of our headquarters within the market locations.
   

  

 This vacancy will close on Thursday 30th April.
   

  

**About Our Offer**
* Personal benefits: Attractive remuneration, discretionary bonus schemes, generous travel benefits, extensive health & well-being support, and more.
* Flexible working: Work is something you do, not somewhere you go. We encourage a healthy work-life balance with a dynamic working environment.
* A career to shape: Access the TUI Learning Hub to level-up and reach your ambitions.
* Broaden your network: We champion intercultural collaboration and provide opportunities to work on global projects and teams.
* Community: Get involved with incredible local charity and sustainability initiatives like the TUI Care Foundation.


**About The Job**
* You'll develop and own the end-to-end automation strategy for Ticketing and Servicing, establishing a clear vision and roadmap that aligns with broader organizational goals.
* Leading a team of Senior Product Owners and Product Owners, you'll manage resources effectively across automation initiatives, balancing competing priorities and allocating team capacity appropriately.
* We'll count on you to identify automation opportunities by analyzing current workflows, prioritizing initiatives based on potential impact, and overseeing implementation from concept to delivery.
* The role involves formulating standardization and automation requirements for key systems including Air Quest, TF-Suite, Air Cruiser, Iris Plus, and Midoco.
* Collaboration with IT teams is essential as you'll serve as the business liaison between operational teams and technical resources, ensuring automation solutions integrate effectively with existing systems.
* You'll develop compelling business cases for automation investments, quantifying potential benefits including cost savings, productivity improvements, and quality enhancements.


**About You**
* You have proven experience in process automation, preferably in a complex operational environment like aviation or travel.
* Experience managing teams in a technical environment demonstrates your ability to lead and develop talent.
* You bring an understanding of GDS systems and airline processes, with experience working with systems such as Air Quest, TF-Suite, or similar platforms.
* Background in process improvement methodologies such as Lean or Six Sigma helps you identify and implement effective solutions.
* Knowledge of API integration and system architecture enables you to collaborate effectively with technical teams.
* Your project management skills with a focus on technology implementation ensure successful delivery of automation initiatives.


 From a workplace to a place to belong. At TUI we embrace diversity, equity, and inclusion, encouraging everyone to come as you are, because together, our potential is limitless.
   

  

 We are committed to supporting candidates with disabilities and impairments so if you require any support, please do let us know.

title: VP Analyst, AI Infrastructure for Head's of I&O (Remote Europe)
description:
**About the role:**
 Gartner Analysts are industry thought leaders who create must-have insights, market predictions and best practices for a broad range of world-leading organizations. A VP serves as a leader within Gartner’s Business and Technology Insights (BTI) group, establishing oneself as a credible voice within their designated market at local, regional and global levels. Utilizing exceptional research and analytical skills, a Senior Director plays a significant role in producing pragmatic and provocative insights which Gartner clients consume and apply to propel their business toward key objectives. They are a trusted source of advice for clients, reinforcing Gartner’s value every day by engaging them via in-person meetings, virtual meetings, sales support visits and Gartner conferences to discuss complex client challenges and offer appropriate recommendations.
   

  

 In this role, you will provide strategic guidance on AI infrastructure to senior IT and business leaders, authoring insights and frameworks to address enterprise AI adoption. You’ll collaborate across teams to integrate AI technologies into on-premises, hybrid, and edge environments, delivering tailored insights to Heads of Infrastructure & Operations and executive stakeholders.
   

  

 Responsibilities include planning and implementing AI infrastructure—covering application architecture, data management, security, and hardware—while developing scalable reference architectures and strategies for diverse industries for insights through producing insights, contents and engagement with clients. You’ll monitor trends in computing, cloud, DevOps, and networking to inform Gartner’s insight and client recommendations.
   

  

 The ideal candidate has deep expertise in AI infrastructure and enterprise computing, with senior leadership experience and strong communication skills. Proven thought leadership and a client-focused, collaborative approach are highly valued.
   

  

 You will make a lasting impact by helping the world’s leading organizations navigate the complexities of AI-driven transformation. And you will find that Gartner will be your best place to be as home in your career, which is proven by some analysts who have come back to Gartner analysts.
   

  

**What you will do:**
* Create innovative, thought provoking, and highly leveraged “must-have insight” content
* Develop new insights and ideas through thought leadership and offer compelling, actionable approaches to client's needs and requests that accelerate the client's ability to act
* Develop in-depth analysis to identify the root cause of a client’s barriers or overall needs and reframe thinking to drive strategy forward
* Demonstrate thought leadership in establishing insight positions across a team of analysts
* Bring provocative, independent insights to Gartner leaders that can evolve the course of a research agenda
* Research, analyze and predict market trends and shifts to provide clients and vendors with actionable insights
* Provide clients and prospects with actionable advice aligned to their designated content area via virtual or face-to-face interactions
* Create and deliver high value presentation materials on and off stage for Gartner events, industry and professional association conferences, and client briefings
* Support BTI and Sales: Provide sales support serving as voice of the market to help Insights teams create content and to drive engagement with clients to make progress against their critical priorities to grow their business
* Provide high quality and timely content peer review
* Build credibility as an industry expert to represent Gartner insights, methodology and strategy
* Actively participate in innovation, ideation, and research discussions and collaborate effectively with peers in the Insights community
* Identify research process improvements or develop new processes that help the team and BTI provide excellent service delivery
* Be a mentor and a coach by supporting more junior team members
* Be client-centric while actively seeking to help clients engage regularly and often with Gartner insights and interactions


**What you will need:**
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience; Graduate degree preferred
* 15+ years of relevant field or industry experience.
* Seasoned I&O Experience for Heads of I&O: Have led Infrastructure & Operations at the highest level in mid- or large-sized organizations, bringing recent, hands-on executive experience, with experiencing AI Use cases and infrastructure requirements across industries and geographies
* AI Infrastructure Expertise: From CPUs, GPUs, ASICs and ARM processors to distributed training pipelines to model-serving architectures, you know the plumbing that makes AI possible. - Technical knowledge of building strong AI infrastructure Strategy and architecture including data ingestion & processing, networking solutions, cloud infrastructure, platforms, DevOps and frameworks.
* Business-savvy strategist: Experience building TCO models, ROI analyses, and forecasts for large-scale AI investments, especially for Infrastructure and Operations
* Integration Architect: Have connected AI infrastructure across cloud, on-premises, AIOps/MLOps, and automation technologies.
* Security and Sustainability Champion: Experience implementing best practices in governance, compliance, data protection, and green computing.
* Demonstrate executive presence; can immediately establish credibility with executives and additional stakeholders
* Strong organizational skills; ability to work under tight deadlines and produce high quality deliverables
* Demonstrate excellence in research and writing ability
* Strong written and verbal proficiency, analytical and presentation skills; ability to engage clients and respond effectively to questions
* Proficient in analyzing and synthesizing data; can effectively apply patterns and frameworks while drawing and defending conclusions to client challenges
* Strong communicator who is able to explain complex concepts concisely and simply
* Subject matter expert comfortable presenting at large and small-scale speaking engagements
* Strong business and financial acumen
* Deep knowledge of the global and competitive landscape within subject area as well as the interplay in that market
* Ability to work independently, while also being intrinsically motivated to collaborate across teams and support the workflow of others, in a multicultural global team
* Learning agile and adept with navigating highly matrixed environments
* Ability to represent Gartner's research methodology and strategies effectively at all levels
* Willingness and ability to travel up to 25% (where applicable)


**Who are we?**
 At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.
   

  

 Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.
   

  

 Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 21,000 associates globally who support ~14,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.
   

  

**What makes Gartner a great place to work?**
 Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.
   

  

 We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.
   

  

 Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.
   

  

 We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.
   

  

**What do we offer?**
 Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.
   

  

 In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.
   

  

 Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.
   

  

 The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.
   

  

 Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Company’s career webpage as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by calling Human Resources at +1 (203) 964-0096 or by sending an email to ApplicantAccommodations@gartner.com.
   

  

 Job Requisition ID:109862
   

  

 By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you have read and agree to the country or regional recruitment notice linked below applicable to your place of residence.
   

  

 Gartner Applicant Privacy Link: https://jobs.gartner.com/applicant-privacy-policy
   

  

**For efficient navigation through the application, please only use the back button within the application, not the back arrow within your browser.**

title: Assistant Manager | Stockholm Flagship Store
description:
**Requirements**
**Description & Requirements**
**Who We Are**
 lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people.
   

  

**Job Summary**
 The Assistant Store Manager role is an essential part of the store leadership team, impacting team member and guest experience every day. Assistant Store Managers are responsible for leading and coaching team members, holding the team accountable to results, and ensuring an outstanding guest experience in the store. Assistant Store Managers also are responsible for accomplishing administrative and operations focused activities, including floor management and scheduling.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities Of The Job**
**Leadership and People Management**
* Create and foster a respectful and inclusive team environment by welcoming and celebrating differences to ensure a supportive and engaging experience for all team members and guests.
* Support Store Manager in building a strong and diverse team that includes team members with various experiences, backgrounds, and skillsets to drive store operations.
* Support ongoing learning and development of team members (i.e., Educators and Key Leaders) consistently and equitably by providing direct feedback, coaching, mentoring, and career path guidance, and addressing performance concerns.
* Support the store’s recruiting and hiring process, onboarding training, and overall performance management activities.
* Engage in career discussions to support and reinforce team understanding of how working at lululemon will contribute to their career and personal growth.
* Provide team member recognition, gap assessment, and overall performance documentation to support and reinforce career and personal growth.
* Collaborate with Store Manager to plan and prepare team member schedule according to labour requirements, availability, and budget considerations.


**Working With Others**
* Establish supportive and productive relationships with all team members, focusing on personal and professional development.
* Collaborate with team members to ensure an optimal guest experience that values guests’ time and support store operations.


**Guest Experience**
* Support team members (e.g., leading by example or coaching) to ensure a great guest experience, including assessing guest needs, providing technical product educations, and supporting in-store transactions and omnichannel programs (e.g., buy online pickup in store, phone sales, and ship from store options).
* Move dynamically on the floor to assess and fulfill the needs of the business, team, and guests.
* Resolve guest feedback and address emergent issues, including guest escalations and emergency requests, helping to “make it right” for guests.


**Operations**
* Implement the Store Manager’s vision for the store and cascade to team members.
* Partner with Store Manager to review business data and metrics (e.g., profit and loss [P&L] statement) to inform planning processes (e.g., quarterly business review and sales planning).
* Partner with Store Manager to manage store’s budget, labor hours, expenses, and P&L statement.
* Provide salesfloor leadership to team members, including planning salesfloor coverage and making decisions to maintain efficiency and effectiveness of salesfloor operations.
* Open and close the store in accordance with the opening and closing checklists.
* Understand and adhere to people safety policies and procedures to maintain a safe work environment.
* Perform work in accordance with applicable policies, procedures, and laws or regulations.


**Budget Responsibility**
* Accountable for delegated aspects of controllable budget or labour hours


**People Management**
* Team lead role indirectly responsible for subset of store employees during shift or as delegated by Store Manager


**Key Skills & Core Values You Bring**
* Inclusion & Diversity: Creates/supports an inclusive environment that values/celebrates differences
* Integrity/Honesty: Behaves in an honest, fair, and ethical manner
* Leadership: Is able and desires to lead, influence, and inspire others; motivates, empowers, develops, and directs people as they work
* Collaboration and Teamwork: Works productively with and supports others to achieve goals; seeks partnerships and diverse perspectives
* Adaptability/Agility: Tolerates uncertainty and ambiguity and can change priorities in a fast-paced environment
* Decision Making: Uses logic and reasoning to evaluate alternatives and make effective, timely decisions
* Strategic Thinking: Sets strategies that are aligned to vision and values of the company to achieve goals/vision/further the mission; considers the 'big picture' implications of decisions
* Resilience: Remains persistent; recovers quickly from setbacks


**Job Requirements**
**Eligibility**
* Must be legally authorized to work in the country in which the store is located
* Must be 18 years of age or older
* Must have proof of the Right to Work and evidence relating to associated local legislative requirements (EMEA only)
* Must have the ability to travel to assigned store


**Availability**
* Willing to work a flexible schedule including evenings, weekends, and holidays


**Other Willingness Requirements**
* Willing to work as part of a team and also complete work independently
* Willing to move through a store for most of a shift to help guests and accomplish work
* Willing to move boxes weighing up to 30 lbs (13.6 kg)
* Willing to work in an environment with bright lights and loud music


**Experience**
* Work experience in leadership or people management


**Job Assets (i.e., nice to have; not required)**
* Education: High school diploma, GED, or equivalent
* Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent
* Experience: 1 year retail or sales specific management experience


 In keeping with our mission, please connect with us at accommodations@lululemon.com if you have questions about how your unique abilities may translate to the requirements for this role, with or without a reasonable accommodation. In your email, please include the position title, the location of the position and the nature of your question or request.
   

  

**Beyond The Paycheck (Benefits & Perks)**
 At lululemon, we care for and invest in the whole person – body, mind, spirit. Our total rewards program is designed to support you in achieving your goals and focus on the things you love. In addition to competitive base pay and a team-based bonus program, we also offer extended health plans, paid time off, savings plans, generous employee discounts, fitness/yoga classes, parenthood top up program and personal and professional development programs.
   

  

 Note: availability of these benefits and perks may be subject to your location & employment type and may have certain eligibility requirements. The Company reserves the right to alter these benefits and perks in whole or in part at any time without advance notice.

title: Innesäljare
description:
På Too Good To Go har vi ett ambitiöst uppdrag: att inspirera och möjliggöra för alla att tillsammans bekämpa matsvinn.
   

  

 Mer än en tredjedel av all mat som produceras i världen slängs. Det har en enorm påverkan på vår planet. 10 % av de globala växthusgasutsläppen kommer från matsvinn och livsmedelsförluster.
   

  

 Genom vår app kopplar vi samman företag som har osåld, överbliven mat med konsumenter som kan köpa och njuta av den till upp till reducerade priser.
   

  

 Som Sales Specialist kommer du att spela en nyckelroll i vårt uppdrag genom att utveckla och underhålla relationer med handlare samt övertyga dem om att ansluta sig till Too Good To Go i kampen mot matsvinn.
   

  

**Rollen**
 Sales Specialist rapporterar direkt till Sales Team Lead. Några av dina ansvarsområden inkluderar:
   

  

**Prospektering & Försäljning**
* Bygga upp ett starkt nätverk av partners genom telefonbaserad prospektering.
* Använda dina bästa argument och förhandlingsfärdigheter för att presentera fördelarna med vår lösning och övertyga partners att ansluta sig till vår rörelse mot matsvinn.


**Account Management**
* Ge regelbunden uppföljning till partners för att säkerställa deras nöjdhet och engagemang under deras första månad i appen.
* Stödja partners i användningen av vår plattform och säkerställa att de fullt ut förstår appen samt integrerar minskning av matsvinn i sin dagliga verksamhet.


**Vi Söker Dig Som**
* Tror på det värde som Too Good To Go-appen kan erbjuda partners.
* Är bekväm med att prata i telefon och har en naturlig lätthet i samtal.
* Gärna har viss erfarenhet av försäljningsprospektering.
* Gillar att ta dig an utmaningar och visar uthållighet.
* Har mycket god kommunikationsförmåga, både muntligt och skriftligt.
* Är empatisk och en god lyssnare.
* Har goda kunskaper i både svenska och engelska.


**Våra värderingar**
 We Win Together
   

  

 We Raise the Bar
   

  

 We Keep It Simple
   

  

 We Build A Legacy
   

  

 We Care
   

  

**Vad vi erbjuder**
* En unik möjlighet att arbeta i ett socialt impact-bolag (och certifierat B Corporation!) där du ser konkret och meningsfull påverkan
* Samarbete med en internationell gemenskap av användare, partners och över 1 100 kollegor i 19 länder med samma viktiga uppdrag
* Möjligheter till personlig och professionell utveckling i en snabbväxande scale-up-miljö
* En inkluderande företagskultur där du kan vara dig själv på jobbet
* Majoriteten av arbetstiden sker på vårt fina kontor tillsammans med teamet, men möjlighet till distansarbete finns.
* En stark, värderingsdriven teamkultur där vi firar framgångar och har kul tillsammans


*A Movement for Everyone*
*We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. With that mission, it’s only natural that we want to build a diverse and inclusive team of highly capable individuals who are passionate about doing things in a better way. We strongly believe we all excel and are more creative when we’re allowed to be ourselves, and we’re committed to a culture where all of us belong.*
*We are an equal opportunity employer and all employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. If you need reasonable accommodation at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.*

title: Bid & Offering Specialist – Cybersäkerhet
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 Placeringsort: Mälardalen, Göteborg eller Malmö
   

  

 Vill du jobba nära affären och vara med och forma hur vi beskriver och positionerar våra cybersäkerhetserbjudanden? Trivs du i samarbetet mellan sälj, teknik och kommunikation – och gillar att få ihop helheten i komplexa anbud?
   

  

 Då kan det här vara rollen för dig.
   

  

 Hos CGI söker vi nu en Bid & Offering Specialist som vill ta ett tydligt ansvar för våra anbud och erbjudanden inom cybersäkerhet. Du blir en viktig del i teamet och arbetar nära både säljare och tekniska specialister för att ta fram genomarbetade, tydliga och konkurrenskraftiga förslag till våra kunder – inom både offentlig och privat sektor.
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 Du ansvarar för att driva och hålla ihop anbudsprocesser inom cybersäkerhetsområdet. Det handlar både om att skriva, strukturera och kvalitetssäkra innehåll – och om att säkerställa att vi faktiskt svarar på rätt saker, på rätt sätt.
   

  

 Du kommer också vara med och utveckla hur vi paketerar våra erbjudanden. Hur beskriver vi vår kundnytta? Vad skiljer oss från andra? Hur gör vi komplex säkerhet begriplig och relevant för beslutsfattare?
   

  

 Arbetet sker i nära dialog med sälj, arkitekter och säkerhetsspecialister. Du behöver vara bekväm med att ställa frågor, utmana, förenkla och ibland hålla ihop många parallella trådar samtidigt.
   

  

**I rollen ingår bland annat att:**
* Ansvara för innehåll och struktur i anbud och upphandlingar
* Räkna på kostnader och bygga business case
* Koordinera bid-processer och hålla ihop deadlines
* Säkerställa tydlig kommunikation kring kundnytta och värde
* Bidra till att vidareutveckla våra cybersäkerhetserbjudanden
* Följa utvecklingen inom säkerhet, regelverk och digitalisering


 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

 Vi tror att du har arbetat några år med bid management, marknad eller affärsnära kommunikation inom B2B. Du är van att samarbeta med säljorganisationer och tekniska team och tycker om att arbeta i gränslandet mellan strategi och operativ leverans.
   

  

 Du behöver inte vara teknisk specialist – men du behöver förstå IT-affären och känna dig trygg i dialoger om exempelvis cybersäkerhet, molntjänster eller mjukvara.
   

  

 Som person är du strukturerad, ansvarstagande och prestigelös. Du skriver bra – både på svenska och engelska – och kan anpassa budskap beroende på målgrupp. Du gillar att ta ägandeskap och ser till att saker faktiskt blir klara.
   

  

 Du är van presentatör och känner dig trygg i att prata inför både mindre och större grupper.
   

  

 Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av offentlig sektor och upphandlingar.
   

  

**Varför CGI?**
 Hos oss får du arbeta med affärer som ofta rör samhällsviktig verksamhet. Cybersäkerhet är inte bara ett buzzword – det är en förutsättning för att våra kunder ska kunna bedriva sin verksamhet tryggt och långsiktigt.
   

  

 Vi är ett bolag där samarbete och ägandeskap är viktiga delar av kulturen. Du kommer in i ett team med hög kompetens och stort engagemang, där vi stöttar varandra och delar med oss av kunskap.
   

  

 Vi erbjuder flexibilitet kring placeringsort i Mälardalen, Göteborg eller Stockholm och formar startdatum enligt överenskommelse.
   

  

 Har du frågor är du välkommen att kontakta Daniel Kommes på daniel.kommes@cgi.com
   

  

**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Sr. Director, Analyst - AI/Agentic Systems in Digital Workplace Applications For MS Copilot (Remote Europe)
description:
Gartner Analysts are industry thought leaders who create must-have insights, market predictions and best practices for a broad range of world-leading organizations. A Senior Director serves as a leader within Gartner’s Business and Technology Insights (BTI) group, establishing oneself as a credible voice within their designated market at local, regional and global levels. Utilizing exceptional research and analytical skills, a Senior Director plays a significant role in producing pragmatic and provocative insights which Gartner clients consume and apply to propel their business toward key objectives. They are a trusted source of advice for clients, reinforcing Gartner’s value every day by engaging them via in-person meetings, virtual meetings, sales support visits and Gartner conferences to discuss complex client challenges and offer appropriate recommendations.
   

  

 As a Gartner analyst within the Enterprise Applications team for IT Leaders and Technical Professionals, you will shape industry perspectives on the future of the digital workplace and critical dependencies (including tools, systems, workflows and skill disciplines) necessary for organizations to transition from early AI experimentation toward realizing measurable, AI-driven business value.
   

  

 Your role involves leading collaborative research focused on major enterprise AI assistant and agentic vendors (e.g., Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Amazon, Anthropic) and their products to deliver actionable guidance regarding their product capabilities, platform maturity, security controls, and integration approaches to scale AI across the workforce.
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
* Create innovative, thought provoking, and highly leveraged “must-have insights” content
* Develop new insights and ideas through thought leadership and offer compelling, actionable approaches to client's needs and requests that accelerate the client's ability to act
* Develop in-depth analysis to identify the root cause of a client’s barriers or overall needs and reframe thinking to drive strategy forward
* Demonstrate thought leadership in establishing insights positions across a team of analysts
* Bring provocative, independent insights to Gartner leaders that can evolve the course of a research agenda
* Research, analyze and predict market trends and shifts to provide clients and vendors with actionable insights
* Provide clients and prospects with actionable advice aligned to their designated content area via virtual or face-to-face interactions
* Create and deliver high value presentation materials on and off stage for Gartner events, industry and professional association conferences, and client briefings
* Support BTI and Sales: Provide sales support serving as voice of the market to help Insights teams create content and to drive engagement with clients to make progress against their critical priorities to grow their business
* Provide high quality and timely content peer review
* Build credibility as an industry expert to represent Gartner insights, methodology and strategy
* Actively participate in innovation, ideation, and research discussions and collaborate effectively with peers in the Insights community
* Identify research process improvements or develop new processes that help the team and BTI provide excellent service delivery
* Be a mentor and a coach by supporting more junior team members
* Be client-centric while actively seeking to help clients engage regularly and often with Gartner insights and interactions


**What you will need:**
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience; Graduate degree preferred
* 12+ years of relevant field or industry experience
* Experience and expertise in:
* The technology and industry market, major vendors and products (e.g., Microsoft’s M365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, Foundry and Agent 365; Google’ Gemini Enterprise and Vertex AI; Open AI’s ChatGPT Enterprise and Agent Kit)
* Digital workplace and end-user computing modernization, with a specific focus on the integration of AI
* Managing AI systems architecture, implementation, integration, and orchestration processes
* Evaluating and/or embedding AI governance and risk controls across diverse business units to ensure secure, enterprise-wide adoption
* Demonstrate executive presence; can immediately establish credibility with executives and additional stakeholders
* Strong organizational skills; ability to work under tight deadlines and produce high quality deliverables
* Demonstrate excellence in research and writing ability
* Strong written and verbal proficiency, analytical and presentation skills; ability to engage clients and respond effectively to questions
* Proficient in analyzing and synthesizing data; can effectively apply patterns and frameworks while drawing and defending conclusions to client challenges
* Strong communicator who is able to explain complex concepts concisely and simply
* Subject matter expert comfortable presenting at large and small-scale speaking engagements
* Strong business and financial acumen
* Deep knowledge of the global and competitive landscape within subject area as well as the interplay in that market
* Ability to work independently, while also being intrinsically motivated to collaborate across teams and support the workflow of others, in a multicultural global team
* Learning agile and adept with navigating highly matrixed environments
* Ability to represent Gartner's research methodology and strategies effectively at all levels
* Willingness and ability to travel up to 25% (where applicable)


 Remote
   

  

**Who are we?**
 At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.
   

  

 Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.
   

  

 Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 21,000 associates globally who support ~14,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.
   

  

**What makes Gartner a great place to work?**
 Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.
   

  

 We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.
   

  

 Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.
   

  

 We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.
   

  

**What do we offer?**
 Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.
   

  

 In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.
   

  

 Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.
   

  

 The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.
   

  

 Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access the Company’s career webpage as a result of your disability. You may request reasonable accommodations by calling Human Resources at +1 (203) 964-0096 or by sending an email to ApplicantAccommodations@gartner.com.
   

  

 Job Requisition ID:107404
   

  

 By submitting your information and application, you confirm that you have read and agree to the country or regional recruitment notice linked below applicable to your place of residence.
   

  

 Gartner Applicant Privacy Link: https://jobs.gartner.com/applicant-privacy-policy
   

  

**For efficient navigation through the application, please only use the back button within the application, not the back arrow within your browser.**

title: Director Business Consulting
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 Vill du leda och utveckla ett växande Business Consulting team och samtidigt bidra till att forma framtidens organisationer och verksamheter?
   

  

 Vi söker dig som vill kombinera affärsutveckling, konsultmässighet och ledarskap – och som brinner för att skapa kundnytta, driva utveckling och bygga starka team. du är den som öppnar dörrar, bygger relationer och driver affärer från första kontakt till signerat avtal och lyckad leverans.
   

  

 Som Director Business Consulting får du personalansvar och blir en nyckelperson i vår satsning på att utveckla en hållbar och växande konsultverksamhet i regionen. Du leder ett team av konsulter, ingår i den lokala ledningsgruppen och bidrar aktivt till vår nationella agenda inom Business Consulting.
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

* Affärsutveckling och försäljning — bygga långsiktiga kundrelationer, identifiera nya möjligheter och driva affärer från idé till leverans.
* Driva nyförsäljning och expansion — identifiera nya affärsmöjligheter, utveckla kundportföljer och ta en aktiv roll i att vinna strategiska affärer.
* Bygga och utveckla kundrelationer — vara en förtroendeingivande rådgivare som förstår kundens behov och omsätter dem till konkreta affärer.
* Ledarskap och personalansvar — attrahera, utveckla och behålla konsulter samt bygga ett starkt och hållbart team med lokal närvaro och global kompetens.
* Kundleverans och kvalitet — säkerställa hög kvalitet i våra uppdrag och ta rollen som Engagement Manager i strategiska leveranser.
* Ekonomisk uppföljning och verksamhetsstyrning — följa upp mål kopplade till tillväxt, lönsamhet och beläggning.
* Partnerskap och ekosystem — bidra till CGI:s samverkan med kunder, samarbetspartners och andra aktörer för att stärka vår position i regionen.


 Du får även möjlighet att vara med och utveckla vårt erbjudande inom Business Consulting mot ett mer strategiskt fokus – med ambitionen att stärka vår roll som rådgivare i komplexa transformationsprojekt och verksamhetsutveckling.
   

  

 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har flerårig erfarenhet av att kombinera affärsutveckling, konsultarbete och ledarskap. Du har ett starkt intresse för att skapa värde för kund och bygga långsiktiga relationer. Som person är du affärsdriven, inkluderande och inspirerande – en ledare som visar riktning och får med dig andra.
   

  

 Vi tror att du har:
   

  

* Flera års erfarenhet från Business Consulting, verksamhetsutveckling eller transformationsarbete.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av försäljning av komplexa tjänster och att driva affärer från idé till avtal.
* Ledarerfarenhet med fokus på att utveckla team och medarbetare.
* Förmåga att förstå olika typer av organisationer och deras utvecklingsbehov.
* Förmåga att kombinera operativ leverans med strategiskt tänkande.
* Relevant eftergymnasial utbildning.
* Flytande svenska och engelska i tal och skrift.
* Erfarenhet av ekonomisk uppföljning är meriterande.


 Vad erbjuder vi dig?
   

  

 Hos CGI får du möjlighet att påverka och utveckla en etablerad verksamhet med stor potential. Vi erbjuder:
   

  

* En strategisk roll med personalansvar i en växande del av CGI Sverige.
* Kombinationen av lokal närhet och global styrka genom vår Proximity Model.
* Ett nätverk av engagerade kollegor inom Business Consulting, både nationellt och internationellt.
* Möjligheter till egen utveckling genom CGI:s globala program och nätverk.
* Attraktiva förmåner, inklusive delägarskap i CGI, pensionslösningar, friskvård och flexibla arbetstider.


**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Retail Partner Account Manager
description:
Hållbarhet och cirkulär ekonomi är hetare än någonsin! Vi på Inrego är experter på cirkulär IT och har varit det i över 25 år. Nu söker vi en driven och målmedveten Account Manager som vill bli en del av vår tillväxtresa. Vi erbjuder företagskunder kompletta lösningar inom cirkulär IT – från återköp (buyback) till försäljning av rekonditionerad IT, kombinerat med smarta finansiella lösningar och andra tjänster.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som Retail Partner Account Manager hos oss blir du en viktig del av vår framgång. Du kommer att ansvara för att köpa och sälja återanvända IT-produkter, exempelvis bärbara datorer och mobiltelefoner, samtidigt som du skräddarsyr hållbara lösningar för våra partners och återförsäljare.
 



  





 För att trivas i rollen bör du vara självgående, målinriktad och uppskatta en dynamisk arbetsmiljö där ingen dag är den andra lik.
 



  





**Dina ansvarsområden** 



* Utveckla och expandera Inregos kund- och partnerbas, med fokus på att vinna nya kunder inom segmentet återförsäljare av IT.
* Bygga långsiktiga kundrelationer och stärka Inregos position på marknaden genom vårt unika ”Retail Ready Refurbished” erbjudande.
* Bygga nya partnersamarbeten med återförsäljare i Norden.
* Samarbeta med teamet för att analysera marknaden och identifiera nya tillväxtmöjligheter.



  





**Vi söker dig som har** 



* minst några års dokumenterad erfarenhet av framgångsrik tjänste- och/eller produktförsäljning mot B2B - direkt eller tillsammans med partners, gärna inom IT eller närliggande bransch.
* erfarenhet av uppsökande försäljning och förståelse för hela säljprocessen.
* ett genuint intresse för hållbarhet och cirkulär ekonomi.
* en proaktiv och resultatinriktad inställning med starka förhandlingsfärdigheter.
* förmåga att anpassa och presentera affärsorienterade lösningar.
* vana att navigera smidigt genom olika system och verktyg.



  





**Om Oss** 




 Inrego har funnits i över 30 år och har en drivkraft att ställa om världens IT konsumtion till att bli hållbar. Vi är ett agilt företag med korta beslutsled och med en kultur där vi löser problem och möjlighet horisontellt och inte vertikalt. Tycker du om att kunna påverka och förändra samt att driva återanvändningen i samhället, så är denna roll helt rätt för dig!
 



  





 Vi välkomnar medarbetare som drivs av ett intresse för vår vision och som kan identifiera sig med våra värdeord: Rak rygg, Glada ögon, Stort hjärta och Mycket vilja. Tillsammans gör vi skillnad!
 



  





 Mer information om oss hittar på vår hemsida.
 



  





**Vi erbjuder dig** 



* En inspirerande arbetsplats där du får bidra till ökad hållbarhet.
* Möjligheten att påverka och vara delaktig i företagets utveckling.
* Konkurrenskraftig lön med grundlön och provisionsmodell.
* Kontor i Täby och Helsingfors samt 170 engagerade kollegor.



  





**Övrig information** 




 Urval och intervjuer sker löpande. Ansök senast 19 april 2026.
 



**Omfattning** 
 : Heltid, tillsvidare (provanställning tillämpas)
 



**Tillträde:** 
 Omgående enligt överenskommelse
 



**Lön:** 
 Grundlön + provision.
 



  





 Vill du bli en del av vårt team och vara med och driva den cirkulära ekonomin framåt?
 



**Välkommen med din ansökan!** 




  





  





 Since 1995, Inrego has been at the center of the circular economy. Our experience has made us experts in reuse of IT.
 



 Being part of Inrego means being part of our vision and the higher purpose that we are working towards. Together, we are changing the worlds IT consumption to become sustainable. Our business concept is to offer organizations the IT solutions of the future through reuse and sustainable services.
 



 Inrego is a privately owned company with clear values and a corporate culture characterized by constant development, openness and innovative thinking. Our values are: Upstanding, Happy Eyes, Big Heart and Strong Will. Our employees describe us as an entrepreneurial company with a can-do mentality, where helpfulness, willingness to change and being solution-oriented are important qualities to thrive with us.
 



 Today we are 170 employees with offices in Täby, Stockholm and Vantaa, Helsinki.

title: Nordic Account Manager - Healthcare Diagnostics Group (HDx)
description:
**Job Description**
**Join a Leader in Healthcare Diagnostics**
 At Agilent, trust, respect, teamwork, speaking up, and uncompromising integrity are the cornerstones of how we operate. These values guide our work as we partner with customers to deliver trusted answers that help bring great science to life.
   

  

**About The Role**
 The
 **Agilent Healthcare Diagnostics Group (HDx)** 
 is seeking an experienced, enthusiastic, and self‑motivated individual to join our successful and dynamic Nordic team as an
 **HDx Account Manager** 
 .
   

  

 This position is based in
 **South Sweden** 
 , and you will be an integral part of the broader Nordic Pathology & Genomics team. You will collaborate closely with colleagues across Denmark, Norway, Finland, and Sweden, sharing insights, strategy, and best practices.
   

  

 As an HDx Account Manager, you will drive sales within the
 **clinical environment** 
 , covering:
   

  

* Cellular Pathology (Histology)
* Digital Pathology (DP)
* Genomics


 Your focus will be to support end-to-end workflow solutions, supply instrumentation and reagents, grow market share, and strengthen relationships with key clinical customers.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Drive sales across Cellular Pathology, Digital Pathology, and Genomics portfolios, including capital equipment, reagents, consumables, software, and service offerings.
* Demonstrate strong understanding of clinical pathology workflows, including:
+ Histological staining, H&E, special stains, IHC, and ISH
+ Clinical genomics techniques such as array CGH and Next‑Generation Sequencing (NGS)

* Proactively identify, pursue, and close new business opportunities in both existing and new accounts.
* Work effectively with Digital Pathology partners to support customer needs and joint opportunities.
* Manage leads and opportunities within CRM, ensuring accurate monthly sales forecasts and territory reporting.
* Develop and execute a strategic territory business plan aligned with market potential and growth objectives.
* Collaborate with Field Marketing to support regional events such as seminars and workshops.
* Conduct product presentations and instrument demonstrations, both independently and in partnership with Product Specialists (PS) and Field Application Scientists (FAS).
* Capture and relay constructive customer feedback to internal teams to support ongoing product innovation.
* Cultivate long-term customer relationships with key hospitals, reference centers, and strategic accounts.
* Actively participate in regional forums and discussion groups.
* Solve a broad range of complex issues using sound judgment and analytical thinking.


**Qualifications**
* BSc or higher in Biology, Biomedical Science, Molecular Biology, Genetics, or related field; alternatively, equivalent experience.
* Experience within clinical pathology and genomics, or strong willingness to learn and develop genomics areas.
* Preferably proven sales experience with pathology/genomics reagents and capital equipment within clinical environments.
* Strong skills in project management, digital tools, and working with cross‑functional teams and business partners.
* Excellent presentation, communication, and self‑management skills.
* Fluent Swedish (written and spoken) and professional proficiency in English.
* Ability and willingness to travel up to 50% of the time.


 What we offer you:
   

  

 This is a full-time, permanent position. Agilent offers core global benefits to all staff.
   

  

 We will make sure you get all the training and development opportunities you need to become the best in your field!
   

  

**Additional Details**
 This job has a full time weekly schedule. It includes the option to work remotely.
   

  

 Our pay ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. During the hiring process, a recruiter can share more about the specific pay range for a preferred location. Pay and benefit information by country are available at: https://careers.agilent.com/locations
   

  

 Agilent Technologies Inc. is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other protected categories under all applicable laws.
   

  

**Travel Required:**
 50% of the Time
   

  

**Shift:**
 Day
   

  

**Duration:**
 No End Date
   

  

**Job Function:**
 Sales

title: Säkerhetsskyddshandläggare
description:
Hos MilDef blir du en del av ett företag som värdesätter engagemang, ansvar och tvärfunktionellt samarbete. Vi söker nu en säkerhetsskyddshandläggare
 **-** 
 en roll för dig som vill bidra till att skydda vår verksamhet, våra medarbetare och vår information genom ett strukturerat och proaktivt säkerhetsarbete.
   

  

**Din nya utmaning**
 Som säkerhetsskyddshandläggare kommer du att stödja säkerhetskyddschef i att koordinera och följa upp MilDefs arbete inom säkerhetsskydd. Rollen är central för att säkerställa att verksamheten uppfyller gällande lagstiftning och interna riktlinjer samt för att utveckla och säkerställa MilDefs säkerhetsskydd över tid.
   

  

**Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter**
* Stödja och samordna arbete inom säkerhetsskydd samt säkerställa efterlevnad av lagar, krav och interna policys.
* Koordinera personkontroller, säkerhetsklassningar, bakgrundskontroller och sekretessavtal.
* Ansvara för registratur och hantering av säkerhetsskyddsklassificerad information.
* Administrera behörigheter och accesskontroll.
* Följa upp, dokumentera och rapportera inom säkerhetsskyddsområdet.
* Bidra i utredningar, revisioner och förbättringsarbete.
* Koordinera utbildningar och informationsinsatser inom säkerhetsskydd.


**Vi söker dig med**
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet av säkerhetsskyddsarbete.
* Erfarenhet av att hantera säkerhetsklassad information och arbeta inom regelstyrda miljöer.
* Utbildning i registratur eller motsvarande erfarenhet.
* God organisatorisk förmåga och vana att kommunicera på alla nivåer.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.


**Meriterande om du har**
* Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet från försvarssektorn, offentlig verksamhet eller annan reglerad bransch.


**Lär känna oss**
 På MilDef blir du en del av ett företag som värdesätter praktiskt engagemang, gemenskap och utveckling av ledande försvarsteknik. Hos oss får du möjlighet att utveckla nya färdigheter, bidra i tvärfunktionella projekt och göra en konkret skillnad för vår fortsatta framgång. Vi uppmuntrar kontinuerligt lärande, samarbete, och erbjuder goda möjligheter till karriärutveckling. Vi är stolta över att aktivt främja hälsa och balans mellan arbete och privatliv. Det gör vi bland annat genom att erbjuda attraktiva förmåner samt hälsofrämjande initiativ.
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om MilDef, våra produkter och vår kultur? Läs mer här MilDef.com
   

  

**Viktig information**
 Vi genomför drogtester på alla våra framtida kollegor innan anställning.
   

  

 På grund av verksamhetens karaktär krävs svenskt medborgarskap.
   

  

***MilDef - WE ARMOR IT.***
*MilDef is a global systems integrator and full-spectrum provider specializing in rugged IT for military, government and critical infrastructure sectors. MilDef provides hardware, software, and services that shield and protect critical information streams and systems, when and where the stakes are the highest. MilDef’s products are sold to more than 200 customers through companies in Sweden, Norway, Finland, Denmark, United Kingdom, Germany, Switzerland, the United States and Australia, and through partner networks in more than 40 countries. MilDef was founded in 1997 and is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 2021.*
**We kindly decline contact from advertising and recruitment sales.**

title: Capital Raising Operations Professional
description:
**This job is with EQT Group, an inclusive employer and a member of myGwork – the largest global platform for the LGBTQ+ business community. Please do not contact the recruiter directly.**
 EQT is seeking a Capital Raising Operations Professional to join our Capital Raising Operations team in Luxembourg. This is a high-impact role focused on managing operational aspects of new fundraises and elevating investor experience. If you enjoy working in a fast-paced, cross-functional environment with global exposure—read on.
   

  

******About The Team******
 The Capital Raising Operations team is part of Fund Operations, a global unit within EQT’s Central Functions. We are looking for an experienced Associate to support new fundraises in Private Wealth Management (PWM) from an operational perspective. The team works closely with several internal and external stakeholders to deliver best-in-class service and provide high-quality support to EQT’s investors.
   

  

 We focus on continuous improvement—streamlining internal processes, enhancing client communication, and leading or supporting various internal projects. We also collaborate with specialist teams across EQT to ensure compliance with investor and regulatory requirements in the alternative fund management industry. This role collaborates closely with teams in Stockholm and across EQT’s global offices.
   

  

******About The Role******
 As a Capital Raising Operations Professional, you will lead and coordinate operational processes tied to new fundraises, with a focus on EQT’s open-ended and semi-liquid fund structures. You’ll be a key point of contact for internal teams and external partners, ensuring smooth investor onboarding, efficient platform integration and a high level of client service. You’ll also help identify and implement process improvements and automation opportunities.
   

  

****Key responsibilities****
* Lead operational sales discussions with clients alongside EQT’s Capital Raising team.
* Coordinate distributor and platform onboarding processes.
* Guide investors through product terms and onboarding documentation.
* Partner with legal and compliance to ensure regulatory alignment.
* Review and support distribution agreements (operational sections).
* Act as the main liaison with transfer agents, managing all trade order types (i.e. Subscriptions/Redemptions etc.) and communications.
* Oversee trading platform setup and monthly trade monitoring.
* Handle investor queries and client service tasks.
* Drive improvements to onboarding processes and digital enablement.
* Contribute to internal projects and operational product developments.


****About you****
 We’re looking for a detail-oriented and proactive professional who thrives in collaborative environments and has a strong service mindset. You’re structured, quality-focused and able to manage competing priorities with confidence.
   

  

****Essential Qualifications And Experience:****
* Bachelor's degree in finance or a related field.
* 5+ years’ relevant experience in private equity, asset management, transfer agency services or a similar role.
* Familiarity with European trading platforms, fund distribution infrastructure, and automated trading processes.
* Strong knowledge of Trade processing (subscriptions/Redemptions)
* Solid understanding of investor onboarding processes within Transfer Agency, including account setup, KYC/AML requirements, and documentation workflows.
* Hands-on experience leading projects, including defining scope, coordinating cross-functional teams, managing timelines, and ensuring successful delivery.


****What we offer****
* A unique opportunity in one of EQT’s fastest-growing areas.
* Work alongside a team of passionate and highly skilled professionals.
* A dynamic, international environment with cross-border collaboration.
* Opportunities for professional development and career growth.
* A chance to shape the operational future of fundraising at EQT.


****Compensation & Benefits Notice****
 We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary, determined based on the role, experience, skill set, and location. Eligible employees may also receive discretionary incentive compensation, awarded in recognition of individual performance and company results. EQT provides a comprehensive benefits offering designed to support employee wellbeing, development, and work-life balance. Benefits include paid time off, parental leave, wellbeing and wellness support, flexible working arrangements, and learning and development opportunities. Benefits are effective from the first day of employment and may vary by location and role.
   

  

**Salary Range Disclosure**
 Compensation for this Luxembourg-based position is competitive and will be determined based on role scope, experience, skills, and local market conditions. The role is also eligible for an annual discretionary bonus.
   

  

****Inclusion at EQT****
 Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing & engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact.
   

  

 Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness; We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.
   

  

 At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative, and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. By doing so, we unlock better collaboration, stronger innovation, and superior investment outcomes.
   

  

******About EQT******
 EQT is a purpose-driven global investment organization focused on active ownership strategies. With a Nordic heritage and a global mindset, EQT has a track record of more than three decades of developing companies across multiple geographies, sectors and strategies. EQT has investment strategies covering all phases of a business’ development, from start-up to maturity. EQT has EUR 266 billion in total assets under management (EUR 141 billion in fee-generating assets under management) as of 30 June 2025, within two business segments – Private Capital and Real Assets.
   

  

 With its roots in the Wallenberg family’s entrepreneurial mindset and philosophy of long-term ownership, EQT is guided by a set of strong values and a distinct corporate culture. EQT manages and advises funds and vehicles that invest across the world with the mission to future-proof companies, generate attractive returns and make a positive impact with everything EQT does.
   

  

 The EQT AB Group comprises EQT AB (publ) and its direct and indirect subsidiaries, which include general partners and fund managers of EQT funds as well as entities advising EQT funds. EQT has offices in more than 25 countries across Europe, Asia and the Americas and has more than 1,900 employees.
   

  

 More info: www.eqtgroup.com
   

  

**Inclusion at** 
**EQT**
 Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing & engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact. Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness; We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. By doing so, we unlock better collaboration, stronger innovation, and superior investment outcomes.
